comment_text.sn34.7:1.4: They cannot “project” the mind to the various psychic powers and higher knowledges (DN 2:84.6).

root_text.sn34.7:1.4: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī samādhismiṁ samādhikusalo hoti, na samādhismiṁ abhinīhārakusalo.
root_text.sn34.7:1.5: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī samādhismiṁ abhinīhārakusalo hoti, na samādhismiṁ samādhikusalo.
root_text.sn34.7:1.6: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī neva samādhismiṁ samādhikusalo hoti, na ca samādhismiṁ abhinīhārakusalo.
root_text.sn34.7:1.7: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī samādhismiṁ samādhikusalo ca hoti, samādhismiṁ abhinīhārakusalo ca.
root_text.sn34.7:1.8: Tatra, bhikkhave, yvāyaṁ jhāyī samādhismiṁ samādhikusalo ca hoti samādhismiṁ abhinīhārakusalo ca ayaṁ imesaṁ catunnaṁ jhāyīnaṁ aggo ca seṭṭho ca mokkho ca uttamo ca pavaro ca.

translation_text.sn34.7:0.3: Projecting the Mind Purified by Immersion
translation_text.sn34.7:1.4: One meditator is skilled in immersion but not in projecting the mind purified by immersion. …”
